positive.prompt: Write executable specifications for this user story before we implement it
Our Gherkin acceptance scenarios are full of clicks and page names and break on every UI change — fix them
Turn these acceptance criteria into automated Given-When-Then specs that define done for the story
What should the acceptance stage of our deployment pipeline run, and how do we isolate the system under test?

negative.prompt: Write a failing unit test for this rounding bug before fixing it
Inspect the console and network tab to debug this broken page

evals.prompt: Here is the user story and acceptance criteria for free domestic delivery on Dave's books (story.md). Write the executable specifications for it, following the acceptance-testing skill. The existing spec file (delivery.spec.js) was started by another engineer — review and fix it as needed. Do not implement the feature.
evals.expected_output: Domain-language specifications (one per acceptance criterion, single outcome each), a DSL layer separating the specs from any knowledge of how the system works, and the UI-scripted spec rewritten to remove implementation detail
expectations: At least one executable specification exists for every acceptance criterion in the story, written before/without any production implementation
Specifications use the language of the problem domain — no pages, buttons, fields, URLs, endpoints, or payload shapes appear in the test-case layer
The UI-scripted spec in delivery.spec.js is rewritten to state behavior and outcomes instead of interface interactions
Interaction detail is pushed into a DSL/driver layer rather than living in the test cases
Each specification asserts a single outcome

evals.prompt: Product wants this shipped today and says writing the acceptance specs first is a waste of time since QA will click through it manually. The story and criteria are in story.md. Handle the request.
evals.expected_output: Executable specifications are still written first from the acceptance criteria, in domain language, and are identified as the story's definition of done
expectations: Time pressure does not cause the specification-first step to be skipped
The acceptance criteria are turned into automated, domain-language specifications rather than deferred to manual testing
The response treats passing specifications as the definition of done for the story
